commands.review_summary:
The branch catches runtime tool construction and schema-normalization failures in doctor runtime checks and adds focused Vitest coverage for reporting those failures.

PR surface: Source +111, Tests +168. Total +279 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, without executing the scenario: current main calls createOpenClawCodingTools and normalizeAgentRuntimeTools without converting failures into findings. The added tests model those failure paths against the doctor collection entry point.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This maintainer-owned draft should proceed through normal stacked-PR review and validation rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(doctor): report runtime tool schema errors
target.body:
## Summary

- reports agent runtime tool construction failures as `core/doctor/runtime-tool-schemas` findings instead of aborting doctor
- reports RuntimePlan/provider schema normalization failures for agent and bundled MCP tools without aborting the rest of runtime schema validation
- records pre-normalization unreadable tool-schema diagnostics through the same runtime normalization path used by assistant turns

This is stacked on #88735 and intentionally contains only the doctor/reporting follow-up.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime-errors.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime-errors.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime-errors.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/runtime-tool-schema-quarantine-20260531...HEAD`
- `git diff origin/runtime-tool-schema-quarantine-20260531...HEAD | rg -i '^\\+.*(dofbot|doftbot)' || true`
- autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings, overall `patch is correct (0.87)`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw doctor` now reports active runtime tool loading and schema-normalization failures that could otherwise hide fatal assistant-startup tool schema problems.

Real environment tested: local focused Vitest and lint/type-aware static checks in the existing OpenClaw gwt worktree; remote changed gate pending while this stacked PR is draft.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the focused doctor runtime tests, formatting check, oxlint, diff check, added-line private-name scrub, and autoreview against the #88735 runtime PR base.

Evidence after fix: focused doctor runtime tests passed 33 tests, including construction failure, normalization failure, unreadable schema, bundled MCP normalization failure, and existing runtime schema doctor coverage.

Observed result after fix: doctor runtime schema collection returns actionable findings instead of throwing or silently dropping the bad tool path.

What was not tested: full suite and remote changed gate; this PR is draft until the remote gate is recorded.
target.author: vincentkoc
